nodes.speaker: obaa_chan
nodes.text: Ah, the farmer rises. The rice was up before you.

nodes.speaker: usagi
nodes.text: The rice doesn't have to carry water from the well, Obaa-chan.

nodes.speaker: obaa_chan
nodes.text: Go tend the paddies. Then bring water to my garden. We have chapel tonight.

nodes.speaker: gennosuke
nodes.text: Warship off the coast this morning. Probably nothing. Probably tax collectors.

nodes.speaker: usagi
nodes.text: Tax collectors don't come by warship.

nodes.speaker: gennosuke
nodes.text: No. They don't.

nodes.speaker: setsuko
nodes.text: Usagi, could you watch Miko for a moment? I need to finish the weeding.

nodes.speaker: narrator
nodes.text: Miko tugs your sleeve. She holds up a small wildflower — purple, with a bent stem.

nodes.speaker: usagi
nodes.text: Thank you, Miko. I'll keep it safe.

nodes.speaker: obaa_chan
nodes.text: When I was young, there was a great church in Nagasaki. Built of stone, with a bell that rang across the harbor.

nodes.speaker: obaa_chan
nodes.text: Portuguese fathers sang Mass in Latin, and we sang back in Japanese. I was baptized there. The water was cold.

nodes.speaker: obaa_chan
nodes.text: They tore it down. The stones are in the seawall now. But the bell — they never found it.

nodes.speaker: obaa_chan
nodes.text: I like to think it's still out there, under the earth, still ringing where no one can hear.

nodes.speaker: narrator
nodes.text: The root cellar opens into a dirt-floored room lit by a single candle. A Maria Kannon sits on a shelf. Six villagers kneel.

nodes.speaker: father_tomas
nodes.text: In nomine Patris, et Filii, et Spiritus Sancti.

nodes.speaker: narrator
nodes.text: The congregation responds in Orasho — a garbled mixture of Latin and Japanese, passed down for decades. Father Tomas breaks a rice cake.

nodes.speaker: narrator
nodes.text: Usagi receives. It is unremarkable to him — what he has always done. He does not yet understand what he is being given.

nodes.speaker: ichiro
nodes.text: Soldiers. Coming up the coast road. Twenty of them. My father says everyone must assemble at the village center.

nodes.speaker: captain_mori
nodes.text: By order of the Regent, all households must register at the local Buddhist temple.

nodes.speaker: captain_mori
nodes.text: Each household head will perform the fumi-e — stepping on the holy image. This proves you are not Christian.

nodes.speaker: captain_mori
nodes.text: This is not punishment. This is protection. The foreign religion brings foreign armies. You have three days.

nodes.speaker: father_tomas
nodes.text: I cannot tell you to step on the image. I also cannot tell you to die. This is between each soul and God.

nodes.speaker: obaa_chan
nodes.text: The martyrs in Nagasaki did not step. The three boys walked to the cross singing. They were younger than any of you.

nodes.speaker: ichiro
nodes.text: Those boys are dead. My family is alive. I intend to keep them that way.

nodes.speaker: setsuko
nodes.text: If I die, who raises Miko? Tell me. Who raises my daughter?

nodes.speaker: ichiro
nodes.text: It's a piece of bronze. God sees the heart. Step on it, go home, pray in secret. Survive.

nodes.speaker: narrator
nodes.text: The room waits. Everyone looks at you.

nodes.speaker: father_tomas
nodes.text: I must leave tonight. If they find me here, everyone suffers.

nodes.speaker: father_tomas
nodes.text: For 250 years your ancestors worshipped the gods of this land. Then the Gospel came, and your grandparents heard something true.

nodes.speaker: father_tomas
nodes.text: Whatever happens tomorrow, that truth does not change. It does not depend on me. It does not depend on Rome.

nodes.speaker: narrator
nodes.text: He gives Obaa-chan a vial of holy water and his breviary. He blesses the community. He leaves through the mountain trail in the dark.

nodes.speaker: narrator
nodes.text: One by one they approach the bronze image. Gennosuke steps on it. He weeps. Setsuko steps on it holding Miko's hand. She does not look down.

nodes.speaker: narrator
nodes.text: Ichiro steps on it. He looks at you. His eyes say: come on. Just do it.

nodes.speaker: narrator
nodes.text: Then Obaa-chan is called. She walks to the platform. She is small. The image is at her feet.

nodes.speaker: narrator
nodes.text: She kneels. The soldiers think she is about to step on it. Instead, she picks up the bronze image, holds it to her chest, and kisses it.

nodes.speaker: obaa_chan
nodes.text: This is my Lord. I will not step on my Lord.

nodes.speaker: captain_mori
nodes.text: I admire your conviction. I wish it were not necessary to do what must be done.

nodes.speaker: narrator
nodes.text: Two soldiers take Obaa-chan by the arms. She does not resist. She catches your eye and shakes her head — barely. She does not want you to die for her.

nodes.speaker: narrator
nodes.text: She is praying. Not screaming. Praying.

nodes.speaker: narrator
nodes.text: She takes your hand. She presses the wooden cross into your palm.

nodes.speaker: obaa_chan
nodes.text: I prayed for this moment my whole life. Not to die. To be ready to die.

nodes.speaker: obaa_chan
nodes.text: Take the cross. Go to the mountain. God will show you.

nodes.speaker: obaa_chan
nodes.text: I am not afraid.

nodes.speaker: the_accuser
nodes.text: She's dead because of this faith. You could have saved her.

nodes.speaker: the_accuser
nodes.text: Your parents drowned. Your grandmother burned. Everyone who loves you dies. Have you considered that you are the common element?

nodes.speaker: the_accuser
nodes.text: Put down the cross. Walk back to the village. Live.

nodes.speaker: narrator
nodes.text: The rain stops. The clouds break. Below, the Sotome coast stretches silver in the moonlight.

nodes.speaker: usagi
nodes.text: I don't understand any of this. But she told me to come here. So I'm here. What do You want from me?

nodes.speaker: narrator
nodes.text: Light steps through the air like a door opening. The mountain falls silent. Even the insects stop.

nodes.speaker: the_messenger
nodes.text: You asked what is wanted. You are asked to go.

nodes.speaker: usagi
nodes.text: Go where? I'm a farmer.

nodes.speaker: the_messenger
nodes.text: Peter was a fisherman. David was a shepherd. God does not call the qualified. He qualifies the called.

nodes.speaker: narrator
nodes.text: The Messenger extends his hand. In it: a simple katana — unadorned, without inscription. The Blade of the Pilgrim.

nodes.speaker: the_messenger
nodes.text: Rome. The year they call 64. The emperor has blamed the followers of the Way. They are praying. Go.

nodes.speaker: narrator
nodes.text: The ground opens — not like an earthquake, but like a door of light. Usagi looks back toward Kurosaki, toward everything he knows.
